description: Launching a workload whose peak load exceeds a non-raisable service quota, causing throttling or hard failure under real traffic.
body: Failure mode: a service is launched without checking managed-service quotas, then hits a function-concurrency, connection, or API-rate hard cap at peak and starts throttling or erroring for users. Detection signals: 429/throttle errors and quota-exceeded events under load; quota utilization at 100%; the limit is documented as non-raisable. Do not assume quotas auto-scale with demand. Safer fallback: project peak load against each quota before launch, request increases for soft limits early, redesign around hard limits, and alarm on utilization. Risk boundary: when projected load exceeds a hard limit, escalate for an architecture change rather than launching into the cap.
